items.claim: Phil Rowe consistently starts fights slowly and performs better in the latter half.
items.quote: Phil Rowe's one of those guys that comes alive in the latter half of of fights, right? He always gets off to a slow start.

items.claim: Oban Elliott demonstrated high-level takedown defense in his fights prior to facing Kofe.
items.quote: I thought that Oban Elliott showed really, really good takedown defense in the fights prior to the Kofe

items.claim: Kofe has previously suffered knockout losses to low-level competition.
items.quote: this guy's got knockout losses to to bums. You know, this guy got knocked out by Sasha Palatnikov.

items.claim: Jean-Paul LeBosnoyani has been training jiu-jitsu since age three.
items.quote: this guy's been training jiu-jitsu since he was 3 years old.

items.claim: Jean-Paul LeBosnoyani possesses a higher level of grappling skill than Oban Elliott or Phil Rowe.
items.quote: I feel like LeBosnoyani's a completely different level on the mat than both those guys.

items.claim: Kofe has shown vulnerability to being knocked out.
items.quote: Kofe has been chin the couple times, man

items.claim: Jean-Paul LeBosnoyani's performance against Phil Rowe was negatively impacted by a 9-inch reach disadvantage.
items.quote: he also had like a 9-in reach disadvantage in that fight, which isn't the case here whatsoever.
